design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels as Predator, defeating Xenomorph enemies through melee combat and special weapon attacks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of health and special attack power meter against waves of Xenomorph enemies
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as the Predator from the films, using iconic weapons (spear, disc, shoulder laser) to survive alien encounters
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately restricts playable character to Predator only despite dual-franchise IP; regional versions significantly rebalanced combat difficulty and ability recharge systems, suggesting developer uncertainty about international audience expectations

difficulty.lives_system: Starting lives differ by region and difficulty: Standard/Novice default to 2-3 lives, Advanced/Expert fixed at specific thresholds
difficulty.checkpointing: Password system planned but unused in final release; level select available through hidden menu input
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple difficulty settings (Novice/Standard/Advanced/Expert) with regional balancing; Japanese version more punishing (reduced HP, health drain on special attacks); International version more forgiving (higher base HP, longer item usage limits, no health drain)

progression.upgrade_system: Consumable items with limited uses: spear/disc (1 use in JP, 6 uses in INT), cloaking device (44 seconds JP vs 17 seconds INT)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Ten sequential stages unlocked linearly; level select accessible via controller input cheat
replayability_hooks: Multiple difficulty settings with different HP/damage scaling
Regional differences in game balance create different experiences
Hidden 2-Player vs mode in Japanese version (Predator vs Charger Alien) completely removed internationally

world_building_techniques: Stage design implies alien hive/facility locations (urban outdoor areas, waterfall basins, creature pods)
Enemy type variety (Charger vs Warrior Xenomorphs) suggests progression through different hive zones

faction_indicators: Predator (player character) vs Xenomorph species (enemy faction)
danger_signals: Alien pods and creature nests indicate infestation zones
Warrior and Charger Xenomorph types represent escalating threat levels
environmental_storytelling: Stage 3 redesign from urban area to waterfall basin suggests sanitization to avoid depicting civilian casualties
Varying stage architecture (facilities, biological chambers) implies progression through alien infrastructure

regional_differences_note: This game exhibits extreme regional tuning: Japanese version 40% harder via reduced HP, health drain on weapons, longer special charge time; International version simplified controls, increased item usage, removed 2P mode, rebalanced palette and sprite designs for authenticity
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Alien vs. Predator SNES documentation)
